sections.content:
The center white square in the 3x3 grid turned green (N)! So clicking with MOUSE changes the color of the cell. The cursor orientation was blue-on-top, skyblue-on-bottom. Let me check what color was applied and understand the relationship between cursor state and what gets painted.
Looking at the image, the center square (row 50, col 30) is now green (N - light green). The cursor had blue on top and sky blue on bottom after the UP action.
Let me check the current state and try to understand the color mapping.

sections.label: TOOL CALL: python
sections.content:
<tool_call>
<function=python>
<parameter=code>
# Check the grid state after clicking
ascii_lines = current_frame.ascii.split('\n')
print("=== Bottom-right panel grid ===")
for i in range(47, 64):
    print(f"  {i}: {ascii_lines[i][22:39]}")

# Check what colors are in the grid now
seg = current_frame.segmentation
print("\nGrid cells:")
for n in seg['nodes']:
    if n['color'] in ['W', 'g', 'N', 'G'] and 47 <= min(r for r,c in n['boundary']) <= 63:
        if 22 <= min(c for r,c in n['boundary']) <= 39:
            b = n['boundary']
            rmin, rmax = min(r for r,c in b), max(r for r,c in b)
            cmin, cmax = min(c for r,c in b), max(c for r,c in b)
            print(f"  id={n['id']} color={n['color']} pixels={n['pixels']} bbox=[{rmin},{cmin}]-[{rmax},{cmax}]")
</parameter>
</function>
</tool_call>

Now I need to understand the relationship between the cursor state and the paint color. The cursor currently has blue (b) on top and sky blue (S) on bottom. The green bar (N) on the right edge is the light green color.

- Paint color: N (light green)
